tasks.title: Review normalization findings
tasks.description: Review all normalization findings and resolve blocking vs non-blocking issues
acceptance_criteria: All blocking findings have a resolution status
Findings are categorized as accepted, rejected, or deferred

tasks.title: Verify ADR completeness
tasks.description: Ensure all required ADRs are present and have accepted status
acceptance_criteria: All ADR IDs from REQUIRED_ADR_IDS are present
No ADR has empty context or decision fields

tasks.title: Approve refined specification
tasks.description: Final approval of the refined specification after all findings are resolved
acceptance_criteria: No unresolved blocking findings remain
Specification is marked as approved
Exit gate specification_approved is satisfied

tasks.title: Generate architecture decisions
tasks.description: Create the full set of Architectural Decision Records from DECISION_DEFINITIONS
acceptance_criteria: All ADR records are populated with context, decision, consequences
Decisions are serialized to decisions.json

tasks.title: Build architecture.json
tasks.description: Assemble the complete architecture document including components, data flows, and deployment units
acceptance_criteria: Architecture document contains all factory and product components
Data flows and security boundaries are defined
Quality gates are listed

tasks.title: Create agent specifications
tasks.description: Define specialized agents with responsibilities, tools, handoff targets, and gate conditions
acceptance_criteria: Each agent has non-empty responsibility, entry_gate, exit_gate
Handoff targets reference valid agent IDs or human_reviewer
All agents have at least one allowed tool

tasks.title: Define contracts
tasks.description: Generate contract schemas for all agent handoffs specifying input/output data structures
acceptance_criteria: Contracts cover all agent handoff pairs
Each contract has typed fields with descriptions
Required fields are properly marked

tasks.title: Generate task breakdown
tasks.description: Decompose the architecture into individual tasks per agent domain
acceptance_criteria: Each agent has at least one assigned task
Tasks have unique IDs following the TASK-XXX-NNN pattern
Tasks define dependencies that form a valid DAG

tasks.title: Generate DAG and validate
tasks.description: Build a directed acyclic graph from task dependencies and validate it has no cycles
acceptance_criteria: DAG contains all task IDs as nodes
No circular dependencies exist
Critical path is identifiable

tasks.title: Define handoff plan
tasks.description: Create the handoff plan specifying which artifacts pass between agents and required gates
acceptance_criteria: All agent transitions are mapped in handoff plan
Each handoff includes required gates and artifact hashes

tasks.title: Run planning gates
tasks.description: Execute all planning quality gates (GATE-ARCH-001 through GATE-ARCH-007) and produce gate report
acceptance_criteria: All seven planning gates pass
Gate report lists each gate with pass/fail status
Architecture is marked as approved when all gates pass

tasks.title: Create users table migration
tasks.description: Create the users table with id, username, password_hash, email, department_id, role_id, is_active, created_at, updated_at
acceptance_criteria: users table is created with all required columns
Primary key and unique constraints on username and email are defined

tasks.title: Create roles & permissions tables
tasks.description: Create roles, permissions, and role_permissions tables for RBAC
acceptance_criteria: roles table has id, name, description columns
permissions table has id, resource, action columns
role_permissions junction table is created

tasks.title: Create departments table
tasks.description: Create departments table with id, name, code, manager_user_id (nullable initially)
acceptance_criteria: departments table is created with id, name, code columns
manager_user_id is nullable for bootstrap

tasks.title: Add manager_user_id FK to departments
tasks.description: Add manager_user_id foreign key referencing users(id) after users table is created (ADR-010 deferred migration)
acceptance_criteria: manager_user_id FK references users(id)
Migration runs after both departments and users exist
Bootstrap script handles null manager_user_id for initial departments

tasks.title: Create document_types table
tasks.description: Create document_types table with id, name, code, prefix, description, is_active
acceptance_criteria: document_types table has all required columns
Unique constraint on code is defined

tasks.title: Create documents table
tasks.description: Create documents table with id, document_type_id, number, title, description, current_version_id, created_by, created_at, updated_at
acceptance_criteria: documents table has FK to document_types
current_version_id is nullable initially

tasks.title: Create document_versions table
tasks.description: Create document_versions table with id, document_id, version_number, file_path, file_hash, file_size, mime_type, uploaded_by, created_at
acceptance_criteria: document_versions table has FK to documents
Index on (document_id, version_number) is defined
previous_version_id is nullable for initial migration

tasks.title: Add document_version_id to attachments
tasks.description: Add document_version_id nullable FK to document_attachments per ADR-006 for version-specific attachments
acceptance_criteria: document_version_id column is added to document_attachments
FK references document_versions(id) as nullable

tasks.title: Add previous_version_id to versions
tasks.description: Add previous_version_id self-referencing FK to document_versions per ADR-007 for version chain traversal
acceptance_criteria: previous_version_id column is added to document_versions
Self-referencing FK constraint is defined

tasks.title: Add UNIQUE(document_type_id, number) to documents
tasks.description: Add unique constraint on (document_type_id, document_number) per ADR-008 for document numbering
acceptance_criteria: UNIQUE constraint on (document_type_id, number) is created
Existing data does not violate the constraint

tasks.title: Create expedients table
tasks.description: Create expedients table with id, number, title, description, status, department_id, created_by, assigned_to, created_at, updated_at
acceptance_criteria: expedients table has all required columns
FK to departments is defined

tasks.title: Create expedient_documents table
tasks.description: Create expedient_documents junction table linking expedients to documents with added_at and notes
acceptance_criteria: Junction table has FKs to both expedients and documents
Composite primary key is defined

tasks.title: Create correspondence table
tasks.description: Create correspondence table with id, number, subject, body, sender_id, recipient_id, document_id, status, sent_at, created_at
acceptance_criteria: correspondence table has sender and recipient FKs to users
FK to documents is nullable

tasks.title: Create correspondence_routes table
tasks.description: Create correspondence_routes table tracking routing history for correspondence items
acceptance_criteria: correspondence_routes table tracks from_agent, to_agent, action, timestamp
FK to correspondence is defined

tasks.title: Create tramites table
tasks.description: Create tramites table with id, code, name, description, department_id, requirements, estimated_duration, is_active
acceptance_criteria: tramites table has all required columns
FK to departments is defined

tasks.title: Create citizen_requests table
tasks.description: Create citizen_requests table with id, tramite_id, citizen_id, data, status, assigned_to, created_at, updated_at
acceptance_criteria: citizen_requests table has FK to tramites
Status column is indexed for filtering

tasks.title: Create oirs_cases with contact fields
tasks.description: Create oirs_cases table with id, number, subject, description, status, contact_name, contact_email, contact_phone, contact_consent, assigned_to, created_at, updated_at per ADR-009
acceptance_criteria: oirs_cases has contact_name, contact_email, contact_phone, contact_consent nullable fields
Validation rule for name + (email or phone) + consent documented

tasks.title: Create notifications table
tasks.description: Create notifications table with id, user_id, type, title, body, is_read, sent_at, read_at
acceptance_criteria: notifications table has FK to users
Index on (user_id, is_read) for performance

tasks.title: Create audit_log table
tasks.description: Create audit_log table with id, table_name, record_id, action, old_values, new_values, performed_by, performed_at, ip_address
acceptance_criteria: audit_log table captures before and after values as JSON
Index on (table_name, record_id) for lookup

tasks.title: Implement POST /api/v1/auth/login
tasks.description: Implement internal user login endpoint with JWT token generation (API-001, M01)
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/v1/auth/login returns JWT on valid credentials
Invalid credentials return 401
Response includes user profile and permissions

tasks.title: Implement POST /api/v1/auth/citizen-login
tasks.description: Implement citizen login endpoint with academic simulation per ADR-004 (API-002, M01)
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/v1/auth/citizen-login returns citizen JWT on valid credentials
Separate token format from internal login
Simulation mode is clearly documented

tasks.title: Implement documents CRUD endpoints
tasks.description: Implement document CRUD: GET /api/v1/documents, POST, GET /api/v1/documents/{id}, PUT, DELETE plus version management (API-015 through API-020, M03)
acceptance_criteria: All six document endpoints return correct HTTP status codes
Version history is accessible via document versions endpoint
Attachments include document_version_id per ADR-006

tasks.title: Implement expedients CRUD endpoints
tasks.description: Implement expedient endpoints: GET /api/v1/expedients, POST, GET /api/v1/expedients/{id}, PUT, document association (API-025 through API-028, M05)
acceptance_criteria: Expedient CRUD endpoints return correctly structured responses
Document association endpoint works

tasks.title: Implement correspondence endpoints
tasks.description: Implement correspondence: GET /api/v1/correspondence, POST, GET /api/v1/correspondence/{id}, PUT, routing endpoints (API-029 through API-032, M06)
acceptance_criteria: Correspondence CRUD works with sender/recipient validation
Routing endpoint tracks correspondence history

tasks.title: Implement portal endpoints
tasks.description: Implement public portal: GET /api/v1/public/tramites, GET /api/v1/public/tramites/{id}, POST /api/v1/public/tramites/{id}/requests, GET /api/v1/public/requests/{id}/status (API-033 through API-036, M07, ADR-005)
acceptance_criteria: Public tramites listing and detail endpoints work without auth
Request creation validates citizen data
Canonical route per ADR-005 is implemented

tasks.title: Implement OIRS endpoints
tasks.description: Implement OIRS: GET /api/v1/oirs/cases, POST, GET /api/v1/oirs/cases/{id}, PUT with anonymous contact fields per ADR-009 (API-037, API-038, M08)
acceptance_criteria: OIRS endpoints handle both authenticated and anonymous submissions
Contact fields are required for anonymous submissions
Anonymous validation enforces name + (email or phone) + consent

tasks.title: Implement reports and notifications endpoints
tasks.description: Implement reporting: GET /api/v1/reports/documents, GET /api/v1/reports/expedients and notifications: GET /api/v1/notifications, PUT /api/v1/notifications/{id}/read (API-039, API-040, M09, M10)
acceptance_criteria: Report endpoints return aggregated data
Notification endpoints support read/unread status

tasks.title: Implement admin procedure-types endpoints
tasks.description: Add GET/POST/PUT /api/v1/admin/procedure-types endpoints per ADR-002 for P-10 screen support
acceptance_criteria: Admin can list, create, and update procedure types
Endpoints require admin role

tasks.title: Implement admin external-entities endpoints
tasks.description: Add GET/POST/PUT /api/v1/admin/external-entities endpoints per ADR-003 for P-11 screen support
acceptance_criteria: Admin can list, create, and update external entities
Endpoints require admin role

tasks.title: Implement RBAC middleware
tasks.description: Implement role-based access control middleware that validates JWT, extracts roles, and enforces permission checks on all protected routes (M01)
acceptance_criteria: RBAC middleware extracts user roles from JWT
Route-level permission checks are enforced
Unauthorized requests return 403

tasks.title: Implement audit trail middleware
tasks.description: Implement audit middleware that logs all data-modifying operations to audit_log table
acceptance_criteria: All POST, PUT, DELETE operations are logged
Audit records include old_values, new_values, performed_by
Read operations are not logged

tasks.title: Create login screens P-01, P-02, P-03
tasks.description: Create internal login (P-01), citizen login (P-02), and password recovery (P-03) screens with form validation
acceptance_criteria: P-01 internal login renders with username and password fields
P-02 citizen login renders with RUT and password fields
P-03 password recovery renders with email field

tasks.title: Create admin screens P-06 through P-11
tasks.description: Create admin panel screens: user management (P-06), role management (P-07), department management (P-08), document types (P-09), procedure types (P-10), external entities (P-11)
acceptance_criteria: All six admin screens render with data tables
CRUD forms are implemented for each screen
Admin-only routes are guarded

tasks.title: Create document screens P-12 through P-15
tasks.description: Create document management screens: document list (P-12), document detail (P-13), document upload (P-14), document version history (P-15)
acceptance_criteria: P-12 lists documents with type, number, title, status
P-13 shows full document detail with version selector
P-14 upload form includes file and metadata
P-15 shows version timeline

tasks.title: Create expedient screens P-19, P-20
tasks.description: Create expedient list (P-19) and expedient detail (P-20) screens with document association display
acceptance_criteria: P-19 lists expedients with status badges
P-20 shows expedient detail with associated documents

tasks.title: Create correspondence screens P-21, P-22
tasks.description: Create correspondence list (P-21) and correspondence detail/compose (P-22) screens
acceptance_criteria: P-21 lists correspondence with sender, recipient, date
P-22 compose form has subject, body, recipient select

tasks.title: Create portal screens P-23 through P-26
tasks.description: Create public portal screens: tramite list (P-23), tramite detail (P-24), request form (P-25), request status (P-26)
acceptance_criteria: P-23 lists available tramites for citizens
P-24 shows tramite detail with requirements
P-25 request form validates citizen data
P-26 shows request status with timeline

tasks.title: Create OIRS screens P-27, P-28
tasks.description: Create OIRS case list (P-27) and OIRS case detail (P-28) with contact fields for anonymous submissions per ADR-009
acceptance_criteria: P-27 lists OIRS cases with status and contact info
P-28 detail shows full case with response thread
Anonymous submission shows/hides contact fields based on auth

tasks.title: Create reports screen P-29
tasks.description: Create reports dashboard screen (P-29) with charts and export functionality
acceptance_criteria: P-29 renders report metrics with charts
Export to CSV function is available

tasks.title: Create notification screens P-30
tasks.description: Create notification inbox screen (P-30) with read/unread toggling, per ADR-001 dual-route design
acceptance_criteria: P-30 lists notifications with read/unread styling
Mark-as-read and mark-as-unread actions work
Dual route (/intranet/notifications and /portal/notifications) implemented

tasks.title: Implement canonical 30-route router
tasks.description: Implement the canonical frontend router with all 30 declared routes, including intranet, portal, and admin sections with auth guards
acceptance_criteria: All 30 routes are registered in the router
Auth guards redirect unauthenticated users to login
Admin routes are protected by role check
404 page is shown for unknown routes

tasks.title: Database migration tests
tasks.description: Run database migration tests verifying all tables, constraints, indexes, and seeds
acceptance_criteria: All migration tests pass
Seed scripts execute without errors

tasks.title: API contract tests
tasks.description: Run API contract tests validating all endpoints against OpenAPI specification
acceptance_criteria: All API endpoints return expected status codes and shapes
Authentication and authorization are enforced

tasks.title: Frontend render tests
tasks.description: Run frontend render tests verifying all 30 screens render without errors
acceptance_criteria: All 30 screens render without console errors
Navigation between screens works via router

tasks.title: End-to-end flow tests
tasks.description: Run critical end-to-end flows: login, document creation, expedient tracking, correspondence routing, portal request
acceptance_criteria: All five critical E2E flows complete successfully
Error flows (invalid login, permission denied) are tested

tasks.title: Coverage threshold validation
tasks.description: Validate code coverage meets minimum thresholds for backend (80%) and frontend (70%)
acceptance_criteria: Backend line coverage >= 80%
Frontend line coverage >= 70%

tasks.title: Acceptance criteria verification
tasks.description: Verify all acceptance criteria across every task are met and documented
acceptance_criteria: Every task has all acceptance criteria verified
Any failed criteria are documented with reasons

tasks.title: EC2 deploy readiness preparation
tasks.description: Prepare EC2 deployment readiness: verify build, lint, test, and configuration for production-like environment
acceptance_criteria: Build completes without errors
Lint passes with zero warnings
All test suites pass
Deployment checklist is complete
